title: Sales Report Manager – Full ERP Web App

requirements.text: Create a Motoko backend actor that stores daily sales entries keyed by date and salesman. Each entry should hold: loadNumber (Text), totalBills (Nat), salesValue (Float), daySales (Float), stockValue (Float), stockQty (Nat). Expose CRUD functions: saveDailyEntry, getDailyEntry, getEntriesByWeek, getEntriesByMonth. Also store purchase/stock records with fields: productName (Text), purchaseDate (Text), quantity (Nat), purchaseRate (Float), totalPurchaseValue (Float). Expose: addPurchase, getPurchases, getAllPurchases.

quotes: Use localStorage to persist all data
Add products to stock with fields: Product Name, Purchase Date, Quantity (pieces), Purchase Rate (₹), Total Purchase Value

acceptanceCriteria: saveDailyEntry stores a record retrievable by date + salesman key
getEntriesByWeek returns all entries for Mon–Sat of the given week
getEntriesByMonth returns all entries for the given month/year
addPurchase stores a product purchase record
getPurchases returns all purchase records
All functions return stable, upgradeable data structures

requirements.text: Implement a dark left sidebar navigation with a navy-blue and white professional ERP color scheme. Sidebar sections: Dashboard (Daily), Weekly Report, Monthly Report, Purchase & Stock, Export Reports. Active section should be highlighted. The overall layout must feel like a desktop ERP application with a fixed sidebar and a main content area. Use navy blue (#1e3a5f), white, and subtle grays as the primary palette.

quotes: Dark sidebar navigation with sections: Dashboard (Daily), Weekly Report, Monthly Report, Purchase & Stock, Export Reports
Professional color scheme (navy blue, white, subtle grays)
The app should feel like a proper business ERP/reporting tool

acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar is fixed on the left and always visible
All 5 navigation sections are present and functional
Active section is visually highlighted
Color palette uses navy blue, white, and subtle grays throughout

requirements.text: Build the Dashboard / Daily Report View. On load, auto-detect the current day of week and display the correct beat (area) for each of the 6 salesman based on the hardcoded schedule below. If today is Sunday, display a 'Weekly Off' message instead of the table. Include a date picker to view/edit any specific day. The salesman beat schedule is: SURAJ (Mon/Thu: BAWADA TOP, Tue/Fri: RUIKAR COLONY TOP, Wed/Sat: STAND TOP), MUNNA (Mon: SHAHUPURI, Tue: SHIVAJI MARKET, Wed: WHOLESALE, Thu: LAKSHMIPURI, Fri: SOMWAR PETH, Sat: MANGALWAR PETH D), CHANDRADEEP (Mon: SHANIWAR PETH C, Tue: STATION ROAD, Wed: RUIKAR COLONY, Thu: SHIVAJI PETH CC AND SHIVAJI PETH A, Fri: SHAHUPURI, Sat: NEW SHAHUPURI), SANTOSH (Mon: LAKSHYATIRTH, Tue: SANE GURUJI VASAHAT, Wed: SANE GURUJI 2, Thu: SHIVAJI PETH B, Fri: MANGALWAR PETH A, Sat: MANGALWAR PETH B1), SAGAR (Mon: BAVADA, Tue: K.BAWADA, Wed: NEW PALACE RD, Thu: KADAMWADI, Fri: MUKTSAINIK VASAHAT, Sat: LINE BAZAR), SUHAS (Mon/Thu: NAGALA PARK TOP, Tue/Fri: MUKTASAINIK TOP, Wed/Sat: MANGALWAR PETH TOP). The table columns must be: Salesman Name, Beat (auto-filled), Load Number (editable), Total Bills (editable number), Total Sales Value ₹ (editable number), Day Sales (editable), Stock Value ₹ (editable), Stock Qty (editable), Total Sales (calculated cumulative). Include a summary row with column totals. Include a 'Save Daily Data' button that persists the entries.

quotes: Auto-detect current day of week and show the correct beat (area) for each salesman
Date picker to view/edit any specific day
Save daily data button
Summary row showing totals for all salesman
Sunday should show a 'Weekly Off' message

acceptanceCriteria: All 6 salesman rows appear with correct beats auto-populated for today's day
Beat column is read-only and auto-filled
Load Number, Total Bills, Sales Value, Day Sales, Stock Value, Stock Qty are all editable inputs
Total Sales column shows a calculated cumulative value
Summary row shows column totals
Date picker changes the view and auto-updates beats for the selected day
Save button persists data to backend (and localStorage as fallback)
Sunday shows a 'Weekly Off' message instead of the table

requirements.text: Build the Weekly Report View. Allow the user to select any week (Mon–Sat) using a week picker or date-based selector. Display all 6 salesman's data for each day of the selected week. Columns: Salesman, Beat, Load No., Total Bills, Sales Value ₹, Day Sales, Total Sales, Stock Value, Stock Qty. Show weekly totals per salesman as a subtotal row and grand totals at the bottom. Use date-fns for all date range calculations.

quotes: Select any week (Mon–Sat)
Display all 6 salesman's data for each day of the selected week
Weekly totals per salesman and grand totals

acceptanceCriteria: Week selector allows navigating to any past or future week
Table shows 6 days × 6 salesman entries
Per-salesman weekly subtotal rows are shown
Grand total row at the bottom aggregates all salesman
All monetary values are formatted with ₹ symbol

requirements.text: Build the Monthly Report View. Allow user to select a month and year. Show aggregated data per salesman for the entire selected month. Columns: Salesman, Total Bills, Total Sales Value ₹, Total Days Worked, Average Daily Sales. Include a monthly grand totals row. Use date-fns for month range calculations.

quotes: Select month and year
Aggregated data per salesman for the entire month
Show total bills, total sales value, total days worked, average daily sales
Monthly grand totals

acceptanceCriteria: Month and year selector is present
One row per salesman with correct monthly aggregations
Total Days Worked counts days with at least one entry
Average Daily Sales is calculated as Total Sales Value / Total Days Worked
Grand totals row is shown at the bottom

requirements.text: Build the Purchase & Stock Management view. Include a form to add new product purchases with fields: Product Name, Purchase Date, Quantity (pieces), Purchase Rate ₹, Total Purchase Value (auto-calculated as Qty × Rate). Below the form, display a current stock table with columns: Product, Opening Stock, Purchased, Sold, Closing Stock (Opening + Purchased – Sold), Stock Value ₹. Also show a stock history log listing all past purchase entries sorted by date descending.

quotes: Add products to stock with fields: Product Name, Purchase Date, Quantity (pieces), Purchase Rate (₹), Total Purchase Value
Current stock table showing: Product, Opening Stock, Purchased, Sold, Closing Stock, Stock Value
Stock history log

acceptanceCriteria: Add product form validates all required fields before saving
Total Purchase Value auto-calculates from Qty × Rate
Current stock table shows correct Closing Stock = Opening + Purchased – Sold
Stock history log lists all purchase records with dates
Data persists across page refreshes

requirements.text: Build the Export Reports view. Integrate jsPDF and jsPDF-autotable for PDF export and SheetJS (xlsx) for Excel export. Provide export buttons for: Daily Report (selected date) to PDF and Excel, Weekly Report (selected week) to PDF and Excel, Monthly Report (selected month) to PDF and Excel, Stock Report to PDF and Excel. PDF exports should use a professional company-style layout with a header, table formatting, and footer with page numbers. Excel exports should use the latest .xlsx format with formatted column headers. All monetary values must show the ₹ symbol.

quotes: Export Daily, Weekly, Monthly reports to PDF (well formatted with company-style layout, using jsPDF + jsPDF-AutoTable)
Excel (.xlsx format using SheetJS / xlsx library, latest format)
Export stock report to PDF and Excel
Integrate jsPDF and jsPDF-autotable for PDF export
Integrate SheetJS (xlsx) for Excel export

acceptanceCriteria: PDF export for daily report generates a downloadable .pdf file with table and header
Excel export for daily report generates a downloadable .xlsx file
PDF export for weekly report includes all 6 salesman rows across the week
PDF export for monthly report shows aggregated monthly data
Stock report exports to both PDF and Excel
All exports include ₹ symbol for monetary columns
PDF files include a company-style header and page numbers

requirements.text: Pre-populate the application with sample data for demonstration purposes on first load. Generate realistic sample entries covering the past 2 weeks for all 6 salesman, with plausible values for Load Number, Total Bills, Sales Value, Day Sales, Stock Value, and Stock Qty. Also add 3–5 sample purchase/stock records. This sample data should only be seeded if no existing data is found in storage.

quotes: Pre-populate with some sample data for demonstration purposes so the app looks functional on first load

acceptanceCriteria: On first load with no existing data, sample entries are visible in the Daily Report for today
Weekly and Monthly report views show aggregated sample data
Purchase & Stock view shows sample product entries
Sample data seeding does not overwrite user-entered data on subsequent loads

requirements.text: Apply form validation across all data entry forms. For the daily entry table: Load Number must be a non-empty text, Total Bills and Stock Qty must be non-negative integers, Sales Value, Day Sales, and Stock Value must be non-negative numbers. For the purchase form: all fields are required, Quantity and Purchase Rate must be positive numbers. Display inline error messages below invalid fields. Disable the Save/Add button until the form is valid.

quotes: Form validation for data entry

acceptanceCriteria: Save button is disabled when required fields are empty or invalid
Inline error messages appear below invalid fields
Negative numbers are rejected for bills, quantities, and monetary fields
Purchase form requires all fields before allowing submission

requirements.text: Design a professional, consistent visual theme throughout the application: navy blue (#1e3a5f) sidebar, white main content area, subtle light gray (#f1f5f9) backgrounds for table rows, dark text, and accent colors for action buttons (navy/teal). Tables must have proper borders, alternating row shading, and sticky header rows for long tables. Monetary values must always be prefixed with ₹ and formatted with Indian number formatting (e.g., ₹1,23,456.00). The overall aesthetic must feel like a professional business ERP desktop tool.

quotes: Professional color scheme (navy blue, white, subtle grays)
Responsive table layouts with proper borders
All monetary values in Indian Rupees (₹)
The app should feel like a proper business ERP/reporting tool

acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar uses navy blue background with white text
Tables use alternating row shading and visible borders
All monetary values are formatted as ₹X,XX,XXX.XX using Indian numbering
Action buttons are consistently styled with navy/teal accent color
Long tables have sticky header rows

constraints: All 6 salesman beat schedules must be hardcoded exactly as specified — do not allow editing of the schedule in the UI
Sunday must always display a 'Weekly Off' message in the Daily Report view
PDF export must use jsPDF + jsPDF-autotable; Excel export must use SheetJS (xlsx) in .xlsx format
All monetary values must use Indian Rupee (₹) symbol and Indian number formatting
Data must persist using localStorage as the primary client-side store, synced with the Motoko backend
nonGoals: Multi-user authentication or role-based access control
Mobile-first or responsive mobile layout (desktop ERP focus only)
Editing or configuring the salesman beat schedule via the UI
Email or SMS notifications for reports

required: Professional business ERP logo mark — navy blue and white minimal icon with a bar chart or ledger motif (app-logo.dim_128x128.png)
